title: Adding the option to trigger an automation based on the workform submitted and not upon any workform submitted
excerpt: Adding the option to trigger an automation based on the form submitted and not upon any form submitted

posts.author: noasegal (Noa Segal)
posts.cookedHtml: Adding the option to trigger an automation based on the form submitted and not upon any form submitted

posts.author: Nachliel (Nachliel from Easyapps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @noasegal

You can set a default Group for the specific form and then create an automation recipe When Item is created and and only if Item is in Group X

If you want to use Status/ Dropdown columns as the flag for your automation, you can try out our monday app Easyfom.

One of our features is Item Default Values, that allows you to set values to the above columns for every item created with the form (when the column is not displayed on the form). Then you can set an automation recipe When Item is created and and only if Status /Dropdown is X.

Download our app Easyform in the monday marketplace and explore our extensive documentation site.

Set up a free demo here that can include help setting up you forms and boards, or contact us at support@easyappshq.com with any question.

Here is an example of our form in action:

I hope this helps,

Nachliel

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @noasegal - What type of automation were you aiming to trigger based on the specific form submission?

posts.author: noasegal (Noa Segal)
posts.cookedHtml:
Updating a date or timeline column and pushing another date 3 years ahead (or possibly setting the timeline to end in 3 years).

This is for a document that needs to be renewed by every contact/item. I want a set of reminders once renewal is needed.
